mission: Make Amazon the default commerce operating system for shoppers, merchants, and advertisers by combining trusted product discovery, Prime-grade fulfillment, scalable marketplace supply, and measurable retail media.

businessModel: Online retail sales across first-party and marketplace inventory
Third-party seller services revenue from commissions, fulfillment, and seller tooling
Subscription revenue anchored by Prime and related convenience benefits
Advertising revenue from Sponsored Ads, DSP, and retail-media measurement workflows
Higher profit density through better fulfillment efficiency, greater wallet share, and stronger service attach on the seller side
marketForces: Consumer ecommerce differentiation increasingly depends on trusted decision support and delivery reliability, not only assortment breadth
Marketplace sellers expect platforms to provide controllable growth, fulfillment leverage, and better commercial visibility
Retail media budgets move toward channels with clearer closed-loop commerce measurement and lower leakage into unbuyable products
Authenticity, counterfeit control, and abuse prevention remain critical because trust failures damage both shopper and seller economics
AI-assisted shopping is changing how broad purchase intent becomes a shortlist and final decision
keyPartners: Third-party sellers, brands, distributors, and vendors
Carrier networks, last-mile providers, and warehouse operators
Payment processors, banks, and tax/compliance partners
Advertising agencies, measurement partners, and brand teams
Manufacturers, catalog data providers, and authenticity partners

products.name: Fulfillment by Amazon and Merchant Operations

designPrinciples: Keep shopper discovery, product detail, cart, checkout, and Prime delivery tightly connected because frequency and conversion move together.
Treat seller growth and fulfillment as one commercial system because catalog health without operational reliability does not compound.
Keep retail media close to catalog, pricing, and inventory signals so ad optimization remains commercially grounded.
Preserve a strong shared trust and policy platform because authenticity, abuse, and account integrity affect every loop.
Use shared commerce measurement across shopper, seller, and ads domains so tradeoffs are evaluated as system effects rather than siloed wins.

recommendedStructure.mission: Own shopper discovery, detail-page confidence, checkout, and Prime-driven repeat purchase behavior.
Own delivery promise quality, order tracking, returns, and consumer issue resolution.
Own seller onboarding, catalog health, fulfillment operations, and payouts.
Own Sponsored Ads, DSP, measurement, and advertiser retention.
Own identity, authenticity, policy, recommendations, and experimentation.

operatingModelNotes: The hardest tradeoff is balancing shopper convenience against seller flexibility and marketplace-policy rigor.
Amazon's moat compounds when Prime speed, search quality, catalog breadth, and seller economics reinforce one another.
Advertising decisions should be evaluated with retail availability and buyability context because media success can destroy value if supply is not healthy.
